chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1624 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For katcha peshi. 14.11.2016. Petitioner present in person. Mr. Nouman Sahito, Advocate holding brief for Mr. Asim Shabir Soomro, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional A.G ASI Bahar Khan Khoso of P.S Islamkot. = This petition is disposed of with the following directions:- Today, the private respondents No.4 & 12-Dhahoo and Rejhu are in attendance, who are put on notice that they will not cause any harassment to the petitioner, failing which appropriate proceedings shall be initiated against them. The learned A.A.G has filed written reply on behalf of official respondents and according to which a proceeding under Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005 is already sub judice before the concerned Court as well as a Civil Appeal No.09/2016 is also sub judice in the Court of learned District Judge, Tharparkat at Mithi. Be that as it may, the parties are at liberty to pursue their legal remedies in accordance with law. However, it does not mean either of the parties, especially the respondents should take law in their hands. In this regard, the police officials should play their role and provide protection to the downtrodden of the society but strictly in accordance with law. The police officials are further directed to provide protection to the petitioner in accordance with law, whenever they are approached by the petitioner with such a request. The violation of this order shall be construed as contempt of Court and appropriate proceedings shall be initiated against the respondents.

The police officials are further directed to provide protection to the petitioner in accordance with law, whenever they are approached by the petitioner with such a request. The violation of this order shall be construed as contempt of Court and appropriate proceedings shall be initiated against the respondents. Copy of this order be forwarded to the learned A.A.G for compliance. JUDGE Shahid
